Class DateRangeView

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.control.Control
com.dlsc.gemsfx.daterange.DateRangeView
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, javafx.scene.control.Skinnable
public class DateRangeView extends javafx.scene.control.Control
The date range view can be used to define a start and an end date via two CalendarView instances.

  • Property Summary

    Properties
    Type
    Property
    Description
    final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty
    applyText
    The text used for the apply button.
    final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty
    cancelText
    The text used for the cancel button.
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Runnable>
    onClose
    A runnable callback that will be invoked when the user closes the control.
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.geometry.Orientation>
    orientation
    Determines how the start and end calendars will be laid out, either next to each other (horizontal), or one on top of the other (vertical).
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.geometry.Side>
    presetsLocation
    Defines where the presets will be shown relative to the two calendar views.
    final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty
    presetTitle
    The name used for the title label of the presets section.
    final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty
    showCancelAndApplyButton
    Shows or hides the cancel and the apply buttons.
    final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty
    showPresets
    Controls whether the presets section will be shown by the control.
    final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty
    toText
    The text shown on the label between the two calendar views.
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<DateRange>
    value
    Stores the currently selected / defined date range.

    Methods inherited from interface javafx.css.Styleable

    getStyleableNode

  • Property Details

    • orientation

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.geometry.Orientation> orientationProperty
      Determines how the start and end calendars will be laid out, either next to each other (horizontal), or one on top of the other (vertical).

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-orientation property. Valid values are: HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL. The default value is HORIZONTAL.

      See Also:
      • getOrientation()
      • setOrientation(Orientation)
      • orientationProperty()

    • toText

      public final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty toTextProperty
      The text shown on the label between the two calendar views.
      See Also:
      • getToText()
      • setToText(String)
      • toTextProperty()

    • cancelText

      public final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty cancelTextProperty
      The text used for the cancel button.
      See Also:
      • getCancelText()
      • setCancelText(String)
      • cancelTextProperty()

    • applyText

      public final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty applyTextProperty
      The text used for the apply button.
      See Also:
      • getApplyText()
      • setApplyText(String)
      • applyTextProperty()

    • presetTitle

      public final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty presetTitleProperty
      The name used for the title label of the presets section. E.g. "Quick Selection".
      See Also:
      • getPresetTitle()
      • setPresetTitle(String)
      • presetTitleProperty()

    • presetsLocation

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.geometry.Side> presetsLocationProperty
      Defines where the presets will be shown relative to the two calendar views. Supports left and right side values.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-presets-location property. Valid values are: LEFT, RIGHT. The default value is LEFT.

      See Also:
      • getPresetsLocation()
      • setPresetsLocation(Side)
      • presetsLocationProperty()

    • showPresets

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty showPresetsProperty
      Controls whether the presets section will be shown by the control.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-presets property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      See Also:
      • isShowPresets()
      • setShowPresets(boolean)
      • showPresetsProperty()

    • onClose

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Runnable> onCloseProperty
      A runnable callback that will be invoked when the user closes the control.
      See Also:
      • getOnClose()
      • setOnClose(Runnable)
      • onCloseProperty()

    • value

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<DateRange> valueProperty
      Stores the currently selected / defined date range.
      See Also:
      • getValue()
      • setValue(DateRange)
      • valueProperty()

    • showCancelAndApplyButton

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty showCancelAndApplyButtonProperty
      Shows or hides the cancel and the apply buttons.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-cancel-and-apply-button property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      See Also:
      • isShowCancelAndApplyButton()
      • setShowCancelAndApplyButton(boolean)
      • showCancelAndApplyButtonProperty()

      Constructs a new date range view.

      Overrides:
      getUserAgentStylesheet in class javafx.scene.layout.Region

    • setOrientation

      public final void setOrientation(javafx.geometry.Orientation value)
      Sets the value of the orientation property.
      Property description:
      Determines how the start and end calendars will be laid out, either next to each other (horizontal), or one on top of the other (vertical).

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-orientation property. Valid values are: HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL. The default value is HORIZONTAL.

      Parameters:
      value - the value for the orientation property
      See Also:
      • getOrientation()
      • orientationProperty()

    • getOrientation

      public final javafx.geometry.Orientation getOrientation()
      Gets the value of the orientation property.
      Property description:
      Determines how the start and end calendars will be laid out, either next to each other (horizontal), or one on top of the other (vertical).

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-orientation property. Valid values are: HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL. The default value is HORIZONTAL.

      Returns:
      the value of the orientation property
      See Also:
      • setOrientation(Orientation)
      • orientationProperty()

    • orientationProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.geometry.Orientation> orientationProperty()
      Determines how the start and end calendars will be laid out, either next to each other (horizontal), or one on top of the other (vertical).

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-orientation property. Valid values are: HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL. The default value is HORIZONTAL.

      Returns:
      the layout orientation of the two calendar views
      See Also:
      • getOrientation()
      • setOrientation(Orientation)

      public final CalendarView.SelectionModel getSelectionModel()
      Returns the selection model used and shared by the two calendar picker instances.
      Returns:
      the shared calendar selection model
      See Also:

      • getEndCalendarView()

      Returns the CalendarView that is being used for the end date selection.

      the calendar view for the end date selection

    • getPresetsLocation

      public final javafx.geometry.Side getPresetsLocation()
      Gets the value of the presetsLocation property.
      Property description:
      Defines where the presets will be shown relative to the two calendar views. Supports left and right side values.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-presets-location property. Valid values are: LEFT, RIGHT. The default value is LEFT.

      Returns:
      the value of the presetsLocation property
      See Also:
      • setPresetsLocation(Side)
      • presetsLocationProperty()

    • presetsLocationProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.geometry.Side> presetsLocationProperty()
      Defines where the presets will be shown relative to the two calendar views. Supports left and right side values.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-presets-location property. Valid values are: LEFT, RIGHT. The default value is LEFT.

      Returns:
      the location of the presets
      See Also:
      • getPresetsLocation()
      • setPresetsLocation(Side)

    • setPresetsLocation

      public final void setPresetsLocation(javafx.geometry.Side presetsLocation)
      Sets the value of the presetsLocation property.
      Property description:
      Defines where the presets will be shown relative to the two calendar views. Supports left and right side values.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-presets-location property. Valid values are: LEFT, RIGHT. The default value is LEFT.

      Parameters:
      presetsLocation - the value for the presetsLocation property
      See Also:
      • getPresetsLocation()
      • presetsLocationProperty()

    • isShowPresets

      public final boolean isShowPresets()
      Gets the value of the showPresets property.
      Property description:
      Controls whether the presets section will be shown by the control.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-presets property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      the value of the showPresets property
      See Also:
      • setShowPresets(boolean)
      • showPresetsProperty()

    • showPresetsProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty showPresetsProperty()
      Controls whether the presets section will be shown by the control.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-presets property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      controls visibility of the presets section
      See Also:
      • isShowPresets()
      • setShowPresets(boolean)

    • setShowPresets

      public final void setShowPresets(boolean showPresets)
      Sets the value of the showPresets property.
      Property description:
      Controls whether the presets section will be shown by the control.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-presets property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      Parameters:
      showPresets - the value for the showPresets property
      See Also:
      • isShowPresets()
      • showPresetsProperty()

    • getOnClose

      public final Runnable getOnClose()
      Gets the value of the onClose property.
      Property description:
      A runnable callback that will be invoked when the user closes the control.
      Returns:
      the value of the onClose property
      See Also:
      • setOnClose(Runnable)
      • onCloseProperty()

    • onCloseProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Runnable> onCloseProperty()
      A runnable callback that will be invoked when the user closes the control.
      Returns:
      the closing callback
      See Also:
      • getOnClose()
      • setOnClose(Runnable)

      Parameters:
      onClose - the value for the onClose property

      • getOnClose()

    • getValue

      public final DateRange getValue()
      Gets the value of the value property.
      Property description:
      Stores the currently selected / defined date range.
      Returns:
      the value of the value property
      See Also:
      • setValue(DateRange)
      • valueProperty()

    • valueProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<DateRange> valueProperty()
      Stores the currently selected / defined date range.
      Returns:
      the current date range
      See Also:
      • getValue()
      • setValue(DateRange)

      Property description:

    • isShowCancelAndApplyButton

      public final boolean isShowCancelAndApplyButton()
      Gets the value of the showCancelAndApplyButton property.
      Property description:
      Shows or hides the cancel and the apply buttons.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-cancel-and-apply-button property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      the value of the showCancelAndApplyButton property
      See Also:
      • setShowCancelAndApplyButton(boolean)
      • showCancelAndApplyButtonProperty()

    • showCancelAndApplyButtonProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty showCancelAndApplyButtonProperty()
      Shows or hides the cancel and the apply buttons.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-cancel-and-apply-button property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      true if the buttons will be shown
      See Also:
      • isShowCancelAndApplyButton()
      • setShowCancelAndApplyButton(boolean)

    • setShowCancelAndApplyButton

      public final void setShowCancelAndApplyButton(boolean showCancelAndApplyButton)
      Sets the value of the showCancelAndApplyButton property.
      Property description:
      Shows or hides the cancel and the apply buttons.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-cancel-and-apply-button property. Valid values are: true, false. The default value is true.

      Parameters:
      showCancelAndApplyButton - the value for the showCancelAndApplyButton property
      See Also:
      • isShowCancelAndApplyButton()
      • showCancelAndApplyButtonProperty()

    • getPresets

      public final javafx.collections.ObservableList<DateRangePreset> getPresets()
      The list of currently available presets.
      Returns:
      the presets

    • getClassCssMetaData

      public static List<javafx.css.CssMetaData<? extends javafx.css.Styleable, ?>> getClassCssMetaData()
      Gets the CssMetaData associated with this class, which may include the CssMetaData of its superclasses.
      Returns:
      the CssMetaData
      Since:
      JavaFX 8.0
